3836.post_title: Best Dog Food UK (2026) - Complete Guide & Honest Reviews

json_ld.headline: Best Dog Food UK (2026) - Complete Guide & Honest Reviews
json_ld.description: Evidence-based guide to the best dog food in the UK for 2026. Covers dry, wet, raw and fresh options with honest reviews, feeding charts, ingredient analysis and pricing.

3837.post_title: Best Dry Dog Food UK (2026) - Evidence-Based Guide

json_ld.headline: Best Dry Dog Food UK (2026) - Evidence-Based Guide
json_ld.description: Comprehensive guide to dry dog food in the UK. Compare kibble types, FEDIAF standards, feeding amounts, storage tips and quality indicators.

mainEntity.name: Is dry dog food enough on its own?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, if labelled 'complete.' FEDIAF-compliant complete dry food contains all nutrients a healthy adult dog needs.

mainEntity.name: Should I soak dry dog food?

acceptedAnswer.text: Soaking helps puppies transitioning from weaning, dogs with dental problems, and elderly dogs. For healthy adults, soaking is preference not necessity.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my dog's food is good quality?

acceptedAnswer.text: Look for: named meat first ingredient, protein above 25% dry matter, no artificial colours, clear ingredient list with percentages, and 'complete' labelling.

mainEntity.name: Is expensive dog food always better?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not necessarily. Price reflects manufacturing costs, sourcing, marketing and brand positioning. Judge by ingredient list and guaranteed analysis, not price tag.

mainEntity.name: Can I feed my dog the same food forever?

acceptedAnswer.text: No nutritional requirement to rotate if your dog thrives on a complete diet. Some suggest occasional variety to broaden nutrient intake.

3838.post_title: Dry vs Wet Dog Food UK - An Honest Comparison

json_ld.headline: Dry vs Wet Dog Food UK - An Honest Comparison
json_ld.description: Honest comparison of dry vs wet dog food for UK dogs. Nutritional analysis, cost breakdown, mixed feeding ratios and situation-based recommendations.

itemListElement.name: Dry vs Wet Dog Food UK

mainEntity.name: Can I switch between dry and wet food?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but transition gradually over 7-10 days to avoid digestive upset.

mainEntity.name: Is dry food bad for dogs with kidney problems?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not necessarily bad, but dogs with kidney issues benefit from increased water intake. Wet food provides this naturally. Follow veterinary dietary advice.

mainEntity.name: My dog only wants wet food. Is that OK?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, as long as it is labelled 'complete' and meets FEDIAF standards. Main consideration is cost and dental health.

mainEntity.name: Does dry food really clean teeth?

acceptedAnswer.text: Chewing action can modestly reduce plaque, but is not a substitute for dental care. VOHC-approved dental diets have clinical evidence.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to feed a dog in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: Budget dry: 1.00-2.00 GBP/day. Mid-range dry: 2.00-3.50 GBP/day. Premium wet: 3.00-6.00 GBP/day. Mixed feeding: 2.50-4.50 GBP/day.

3839.post_title: Best Puppy Food UK (2026) - Growth-Stage Nutrition Guide

json_ld.headline: Best Puppy Food UK (2026) - Growth-Stage Nutrition Guide
json_ld.description: Complete guide to feeding puppies in the UK. Age-based schedules, FEDIAF nutrient requirements, portion charts, transition timelines and large breed considerations.

mainEntity.name: How often should I feed my puppy?

acceptedAnswer.text: 6-12 weeks: 4 meals/day. 3-6 months: 3 meals/day. 6-12 months: 2 meals/day. After 12 months: 1-2 meals/day.

mainEntity.name: When should I switch from puppy to adult food?

acceptedAnswer.text: Small breeds: 9-12 months. Medium breeds: 12-14 months. Large breeds: 14-18 months. Giant breeds: 18-24 months.

mainEntity.name: Can I feed my puppy adult dog food?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not recommended. Puppy food has higher protein, fat, calcium and DHA for growth. Adult food may not support proper development.

mainEntity.name: How much should I feed my puppy?

acceptedAnswer.text: Follow manufacturer guidelines based on expected adult weight. Adjust based on body condition score. Overfeeding is more common than underfeeding.

mainEntity.name: Is raw food safe for puppies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Raw feeding for puppies is controversial. Puppies have developing immune systems. If choosing raw, ensure it meets FEDIAF puppy standards and consult your vet.

mainEntity.name: Do large breed puppies need special food?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Large breed puppy food has controlled calcium (0.8-1.2%) and energy density to prevent rapid growth which can cause skeletal problems.
